content.description:
**Status:** Merged into notes

## Summary

Structured notes are now part of the regular note type. Any note can have `content.computed` fields and `{{field-name}}` references in its `content.description`. Uses [Reactive Computed Fields](item://c0c0c0c0-0074-0000-0000-000000000000) as the single underlying mechanism ‚Äî a transclusion is just a computed field whose formula mirrors another item's field. Notes without computed fields render exactly as before.

## The Unification

The original version of this proposal introduced four Hob page primitives (`text`, `transclude`, `val`, `link`) and a new page format where pages are Hob expressions. Through iteration, a simpler insight emerged: all of these collapse into one mechanism ‚Äî computed fields with `get-field` as uniform access.

| What you want | What it actually is |
|---|---|
| Transclusion of another note | `(get-field @other-note :description)` ‚Äî a computed field mirroring another item's field |
| Inline computed value | `(* (get-field :self :quantity) (get-field :self :price))` ‚Äî a computed field with logic |
| Functional transclusion (`?call=`) | `(invoke (require \"module\") \"fnName\" api params)` ‚Äî a computed field calling a module export |
| Aggregation | `(reduce + 0 (map (fn [i] (get-field i :amount)) (query-tagged @expense-tag)))` ‚Äî a computed field over a query |

One mechanism. One reactive system. One way to track dependencies. No special transclusion primitives needed.

## Note Structure

Any note (type `871ae771-...`) can use computed fields:

```json
{
  "type": "871ae771-b9b1-4f40-8c7f-d9038bfb69c3",
  "content": {
    "description": "Here is a summary: {{summary}}. The total budget is **{{total-budget}}**.",
    "quantity": 10,
    "unit-price": 25.50,
    "computed": {
      "summary": ["get-field", "@other-note", ":description"],
      "total-budget": ["*", ["get-field", ":self", ":quantity"],
                            ["get-field", ":self", ":unit-price"]]
    }
  }
}
```

- `content.description` ‚Äî markdown string with `{{field-name}}` references. Uses the standard note field.
- `content.computed` ‚Äî map of field names to Hob formulas. Evaluated lazily via `get-field`. See [Reactive Computed Fields](item://c0c0c0c0-0074-0000-0000-000000000000).
- Static fields (`quantity`, `unit-price`) coexist alongside computed fields normally.

## Authoring Experience

The editor preserves the feel of writing a note:

- **Prose**: type normally in CodeMirror ‚Äî markdown, same as today.
- **Field references**: insert `{{field-name}}` anywhere in the body to reference a named field (static or computed).
- **Links**: use existing `item://id` syntax ‚Äî unchanged.
- **Field management**: a side panel or inspector for creating and editing computed fields ‚Äî name, Hob formula, current value.

One syntax, one concept: `{{field-name}}` always means "resolve this field via `get-field`." Whether the field is a transclusion, a calculation, or a static value is defined in the field itself, not in the body syntax.

### Editor gestures

Inserting a transclusion is two logical steps ‚Äî create the computed field, then reference it ‚Äî but the editor streamlines this into a single gesture: an "insert transclusion" action prompts for a target item and a field name, then creates both the `content.computed` entry and the `{{name}}` reference at the cursor.

### Storage

What you see in the editor is what's stored. `{{field-name}}` in the body maps 1:1 to a key in `content.computed` (or a static field in `content`). No compilation step, no generated names, trivial round-tripping.

## Rendering

The note readonly view (a generic-view delegate) renders via `field-view-markdown-readonly`, which:

1. Detects `{{field-name}}` patterns in `content.description`.
2. For each reference, calls `api.getField(itemId, fieldName)` ‚Äî a JS API that delegates to the Hob `get-field` function.
3. Wraps resolved values in `<span class=\"field-ref\">` with dashed underline styling and click-to-scroll behavior.
4. Renders the result as markdown via `hobson-markdown`.

A collapsible \"Fields\" section (rendered by `field-view-field-summary`) shows:
- Static fields as `name: value`
- Computed fields with formula (via `field-view-hob-structural`), resolved value, and provenance links to referenced items.

For notes without `{{` references or computed fields, neither the ref resolution nor the field summary activates ‚Äî rendering is identical to before.

## Implementation Progress

### Completed

- **Merged into note type**: Structured note capabilities are now part of the regular note type (`871ae771-...`). The note readonly and editable views handle computed fields, static fields, and `{{field-name}}` references.
- **`get-field` function** in the Hob interpreter: unified field access with computed field support. Resolves item refs, keywords, and string field names. Checks `content.computed` for formulas, evaluates via Hob evaluator, falls back to item root then `content`. Includes cycle detection via `_computingFields` Set.
- **`api.getField`** on the rendering API: JS-callable wrapper around Hob `get-field`, enabling field views and generic-view delegates to resolve computed fields without needing the Hob interpreter directly.
- **`resolve-field-refs`** in Hob stdlib: resolves `{{field-name}}` references in a string using `get-field` ‚Äî used for cross-item body transclusion.
- **`field-view-markdown-readonly`**: Updated to detect `{{field-name}}` patterns, resolve them via `api.getField`, wrap resolved values in clickable `<span class=\"field-ref\">` with dashed underline styling and click-to-scroll behavior. No effect on notes without `{{` references.
- **`field-view-field-summary`**: Readonly combined summary showing static fields and computed fields. Computed fields display formula (via `field-view-hob-structural`), resolved value, and provenance links to referenced items (extracted via JSON AST walk).
- **`field-view-computed-fields`** (`e8d7c6b5-...-5c4e`): Editable field view rendering `content.computed` as name/formula pairs. Each formula edited via `field-view-hob-structural`. Supports add/remove/rename.
- **`field-view-static-fields`** (`e8d7c6b5-...-5c4f`): Editable field view for arbitrary static content fields, with caller-specified `exclude` list.
- **`generic-view`** updated: passes `item` to field views via options; skips field views that render as `display: none`.
- **Provenance links**: Computed field formulas are walked (JSON AST) to extract `@`-prefixed item references. Each referenced item is displayed as a clickable link in the field summary, using `api.openItem` for navigation.\n- **Functional transclusions**: Computed fields can call module exports that return DOM elements. `require` moved from view-only to stdlib (available in all Hob contexts). The rendering `api` is passed into the formula evaluation environment via `_evalApi`, so formulas can call `(invoke (require \"module\") \"fn\" api params)`. DOM results are mounted into placeholder spans after markdown rendering.\n- **`require` in stdlib**: Moved from `registerViewOps` to `registerItemOps` ‚Äî now available in computed field evaluation, REPL, event handlers, not just views. Uses `api.require` from the kernel module system.

### Known Limitations

- **Cross-item reactivity**: The view only re-renders when its own item changes. If a computed field references another item and that item changes, the view doesn't re-render. See [Reactive Computed Fields](item://c0c0c0c0-0074-0000-0000-000000000000) for the assessment (~85 lines to fix).
- **No caching**: Computed fields are re-evaluated on every access. Fine for current use; caching is a future optimization.
- **No error sentinels**: Formula errors are caught and shown as empty or `[error]`. The proposal describes error items and `ERR` links ‚Äî not yet implemented (~40 lines). See [Reactive Computed Fields](item://c0c0c0c0-0074-0000-0000-000000000000).
- **Existing structured notes need migration**: Items created with the old `structured-note` type and `content.body` need their type changed to the note type and `content.body` renamed to `content.description`.

### Next Steps

- Migrate existing structured-note items to the note type.
- Author real notes to evaluate the experience.
- Consider cross-item reactivity improvements if the experiment proves valuable.

## Experiment Plan

1. ~~Create the `structured-note` type definition.~~ Done.
2. ~~Implement the [Reactive Computed Fields](item://c0c0c0c0-0074-0000-0000-000000000000) mechanism.~~ Done (basic `get-field` with cycle detection; caching deferred).
3. ~~Build a basic view and editor for structured notes.~~ Done.
4. ~~Add provenance links.~~ Done (AST inspection approach).
5. ~~Merge into the note type.~~ Done ‚Äî note views now handle computed fields, `{{refs}}`, and field summary.
6. Author real notes to evaluate the experience.
7. Evaluate: does this add meaningful value over plain markdown notes?

## Provenance and Linking (Resolved)

Implemented using **approach A: AST inspection**. The `field-view-field-summary` walks each computed field's JSON AST to find `@`-prefixed item references. Each referenced item is displayed as a clickable link below the formula, using `api.openItem` for navigation with modifier key support (Ctrl/Cmd = new tab, Alt = root, plain = attach).

This covers the common cases:
- `(get-field @item :field)` ‚Äî link to the referenced item
- `(some-function @item)` ‚Äî link to the target item
- Complex formulas with multiple refs ‚Äî all referenced items shown
- Self-referential formulas ‚Äî no external links

Function provenance (linking to the code item that defines a function) is not yet implemented ‚Äî it would require `_symbols` metadata search, which can be added later if needed.

## What This Enables

- **Reactive transclusions** that update when the source changes ‚Äî same `get-field` mechanism as any computed field.
- **Live computed values** inline in prose ‚Äî item counts, field references, aggregations, projections.
- **Functional transclusions** ‚Äî applying a function to an item and embedding the result, replacing the `?call=` URL parameter convention with a proper computed field.
- **Queryable references** ‚Äî computed field formulas are JSON AST, walkable and inspectable without parsing markdown.
- **Spreadsheet-like calculations** across items ‚Äî budgets, projections, derived metrics.
- **One mechanism** for all reactive data ‚Äî no separate systems for transclusions, computed values, and aggregations.

## What This Does Not Do

- **Break existing notes.** Notes without `{{` references or `content.computed` render exactly as before.
- **Replace markdown for prose.** Bold, italic, headings, lists ‚Äî still markdown, still rendered by markdown-it.
- **Require learning Hob to write notes.** Writing Hob formulas is opt-in for computed values. Simple notes are still simple.
- **Change the item model.** Items still have `content` maps. `content.computed` is a new convention, not a schema change.

## References

- [Reactive Computed Fields](item://c0c0c0c0-0074-0000-0000-000000000000) ‚Äî The underlying mechanism: computed fields, `get-field`, caching, reactivity, cycle detection.
- [Hob Language Design Direction](item://c0c0c0c0-0058-0000-0000-000000000000) ‚Äî Hob as the primary user-programming language.
- [Rich Transclusion Support](item://c0c0c0c0-0035-0000-0000-000000000000) ‚Äî The existing transclusion system in markdown notes.
